concerns.text: FR-002 requires alignment and variant calling. T013 (Generate simulated FASTQ) is marked [X] (complete) but the independent verifier rejected it for missing outputs (truncated script, missing FASTQ files). Without a working T013, FR-002 cannot be satisfied on the synthetic path, leaving the primary execution strategy broken.

FR-007 requires a likelihood-ratio test for PRS. T026 (Integration test for PRS validation) is marked [X] (complete) but the independent verifier explicitly stated the artifact `tests/integration/test_prs_validation.py` does not exist. The verification step for FR-007 is missing.

T024 is marked [X] (complete) but the description states it is 'Deprecated logic moved to T043'. A task cannot be 'complete' if it is deprecated and its logic has been moved; this creates a false sense of coverage for the functionality it was supposed to represent.

Tasks T034 (Code cleanup), T036 (Edge case tests), T037 (Quickstart validation), and T041 (Profiling) are marked [X] (complete) while the independent verifier confirmed their artifacts are missing or invalid. These tasks are essential for the 'Polish' phase and reproducibility; marking them complete without artifacts leaves the implementation plan unverified.

T045 implements a power analysis check before US2. However, T043 already enforces the power analysis halt before US1 (GWAS). The spec (FR-012) requires a single power analysis halt. T045 appears to be a redundant implementation of a constraint already covered by T043, creating potential for conflicting halt logic or unnecessary re-computation.
